summary: Frontend validation of three 'Big Vision' features on ClosetAI mobile (Expo web). All three PASS end-to-end. (1) Style Avatar Onboarding: new signup auto-routed to /onboarding, all 5 steps render (intro -> body -> style -> color -> location -> finish), Continue advances each step, style chips toggle correctly (verified `styles` rename to `selectedStyles` — StyleSheet 'styles' resolves with no runtime errors), Finish PATCHed /api/profile with onboarded:true and redirected away from /onboarding (landed on /wardrobe). (2) Outfits/Stylist tab: outfits-screen renders, all 9 preset chips (work/weekend/date_night/travel/wedding/casual/formal/hot_weather/cold_weather) found, auto-weather-pill rendered (25°C · mostly clear via getCurrentCoords fallback), tapping outfit-gen-work appended user message and AI reply card (POST /api/outfit/generator returned successfully). (3) Build Around This Item: 'Build Around This Item' pill button visible above 'Save Changes' on wardrobe item detail; tap opened slide-up modal with anchor thumbnail + 'CLOSETAI · BUILD AROUND' kicker + loading state, AI reply text rendered within ~15s, 2 build-rec-* recommended items shown in horizontal scroll, build-modal-close closed the modal cleanly. Minor non-blocking console warnings observed (shadow* deprecated, props.pointerEvents deprecated, useNativeDriver fallback) — these are RN-web warnings, not bugs. No deprecated Expo packages (expo-av/expo-barcode-scanner/expo-background-fetch) detected in onboarding/outfits/wardrobe-detail files.

minor.issue: Returns 500 with AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'get' at /app/backend/server.py:537 in _wish_doc_to_model when called after the wishlist item was just deleted (race / stale id). Unrelated to Big Vision features but discovered while tailing backend logs. Add a None guard before unpacking the doc.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning: 'shadow*' style props are deprecated, use 'boxShadow'. Source: shared theme/components using shadows.soft (e.g. /app/frontend/app/onboarding.tsx:493, /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/outfits.tsx:434). Non-blocking on native; only noisy on web.
Console warning: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. Originates from a third-party RN component on web; non-blocking.

Console warning on Modal slide-up: 'useNativeDriver is not supported because the native animated module is missing. Falling back to JS-based animation.' This is expected on web preview, but visible at /app/frontend/app/wardrobe/[id].tsx Modal (animationType='slide').

action_items: Optional: migrate 'shadow*' style props to 'boxShadow' in /app/frontend/src/theme.ts to remove RN-web deprecation warnings.
No functional fixes required — all three Big Vision features are working as specified.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/app/onboarding.tsx is 517 lines (under 700 threshold) — OK. selectedStyles rename verified at line 52; styles.hero/heroVeil/heroText resolve cleanly to the StyleSheet at line 434.
/app/frontend/app/wardrobe/[id].tsx is 449 lines — OK. buildAround() at line 90 correctly falls back from profile coords to getCurrentCoords and gracefully handles errors.
/app/frontend/app/(tabs)/outfits.tsx outfitChat call passes lat/lon (line 92-93) — matches backend signature.
seed_data_creation: Created 2 TEST_ prefixed wardrobe items for test@closetai.com (TEST_top_0, TEST_bottom_1) to exercise Build Around. Both DELETED via /api/wardrobe/{id} after the test. Also created an ephemeral QA signup user (qa<timestamp>@closetai.com / qa12345) to verify auto-route to /onboarding for users with onboarded:false.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend Big Vision (onboarding + outfit presets + build-around) is fully working. Test user (test@closetai.com) originally had 0 wardrobe items despite earlier notes — if future tests need wardrobe data, seed via POST /api/wardrobe. Backend was previously validated 100% in iteration_3.json; no regressions seen during this round.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no bugs found. Pre-existing TypeScript fix (`styles` -> `selectedStyles`) is correctly applied and the StyleSheet object is no longer shadowed by state, so styles.hero/heroVeil/heroText/etc. render correctly at runtime.
